freshnessExpectation: Core metrics are current only after a successful TapTouch sync. Historical context depends on cached scrape snapshots and stored day-level order history.

sourceInventory.name: TapTouch Backoffice
sourceInventory.useFor: Sales summaries, orders, receipts, product performance

sourceInventory.name: Local scrape snapshot
sourceInventory.useFor: Fallback reads, historical order cache, product snapshots

sourceInventory.name: Dashboard API layer
sourceInventory.useFor: Canonical website metrics, product analysis payloads, date-based order lookups

keyMetrics.name: Total revenue
keyMetrics.definition: Gross sales amount shown in the dashboard summary for the active business date.
keyMetrics.caveats: Depends on the latest successful TapTouch sync and local timezone handling.

keyMetrics.name: Total orders
keyMetrics.definition: Count of non-summary orders returned for the active business date.
keyMetrics.caveats: Summary-like rows are filtered out before API mapping.

keyMetrics.name: Average ticket
keyMetrics.definition: Total revenue divided by total orders for the selected date.
keyMetrics.caveats: Should be treated as derived, not independently sourced.

keyMetrics.name: Peak hour revenue
keyMetrics.definition: Highest hourly revenue bucket within the current day timeline.
keyMetrics.caveats: Requires a populated hourly sales array.

keyMetrics.name: Product revenue and profit
keyMetrics.definition: Per-SKU sales amount and estimated profit returned by the product analysis workflow.
keyMetrics.caveats: Profit quality depends on the upstream product report cost fields.

keyMetrics.name: Receipt cache coverage
keyMetrics.definition: Number of orders whose detailed receipt has already been fetched and cached locally.
keyMetrics.caveats: Coverage increases over time because receipt detail is fetched on demand and via background workers.

queryPatterns.name: Daily store pulse
queryPatterns.useWhen: Checking how the restaurant performed today.

queryPatterns.name: Historical order audit
queryPatterns.useWhen: Reviewing a previous day or validating a specific order list.

queryPatterns.name: Menu performance analysis
queryPatterns.useWhen: Ranking SKUs, checking concentration risk, and spotting growth or decline.

gotchas: The repo currently uses cached local JSON plus live TapTouch sync, so stale cache and fresh sync can briefly disagree.
Historical depth is strongest for orders that have already been fetched or cached; not every past date has the same coverage.
There is no warehouse connector yet, so future Data Analytics work should treat this layer as app-semantic guidance rather than full source-of-truth validation.
openQuestions: Whether product category mappings should be normalized into a dedicated dimension instead of relying on report labels.
Whether store labor, inventory, and promotion context should become additional source lanes for broader operational analysis.
Whether long-term weekly and monthly retention of scrape snapshots should be formalized for trend reporting.
futurePrompts: @Data Analytics diagnose why today's revenue is above or below this week's average for PROSPERITY XH.
@Data Analytics analyze the top product concentration risk in PROSPERITY XH and recommend what to promote next.
@Data Analytics review receipt cache coverage and tell me what data is still missing for a complete order audit.
